title: DCP Plugin Complete Outage Fix (missing @anthropic-ai/tokenizer)
summary: Restore the @tarquinen/opencode-dcp plugin to a loading state. It fails on EVERY opencode launch with `Cannot find module '@anthropic-ai/tokenizer'` because the cached 3.1.13 install never hoisted that transitive dep (it IS declared in the plugin's package.json). Fix: clear the stale cache and clean-install 3.1.14 (durable), or manually install @anthropic-ai/tokenizer@0.0.4 into the cache node_modules (fallback). Scope is DCP-ONLY: does NOT touch the runtime, SQLite DB, scheduler, or opencode.jsonc. The separate session_message.seq FATAL (runtime 1.15.10) remains out of scope and tracked by 20260628-opencode-session-message-seq-fatal.
keyOutcomes: Root-caused the outage: DCP 3.1.13 cache install missing @anthropic-ai/tokenizer (declared dep, never hoisted) -> import at dist/index.js:1601 throws -> plugin fails to load.
Confirmed live on 2026-06-29 (log 2026-06-29T150002.log) - identical to the 2026-06-28 finding; the June 28 seq-fatal track never executed (aborted Phase 0.1), so DCP was never fixed.
Plan is DCP-only and low-risk: only edits files under the plugin cache dir; verification ignores the unrelated seq crash.
risks: npm install may reproduce the incomplete-hoist bug -> Phase 2 manually installs the tokenizer.
Verifying via opencode run hits the unrelated seq FATAL (non-zero exit) -> Phase 3 ignores exit code and gates only on DCP-load lines.
Future cache re-resolve could drop a Phase-2 manual fix -> Phase 1 clean 3.1.14 install is the durable path.
validationCriteria: AC-1: @anthropic-ai/tokenizer package.json exists in the plugin cache node_modules.
AC-2: newest launch log has a service=plugin path=@tarquinen/opencode-dcp line and NO failed-to-load / tokenizer error.
AC-3: pre-fix cache backup dir exists and is non-empty.
AC-4: dcp.jsonc parses; active model zai-coding-plan/glm-5.2 cap = 150000.
AC-5: metadata.json completed/100%; tracks.md row=complete; execution-log has Execution Complete entry.
